messages.content: You digest raw market-research search results into citable claim atoms for a risk-hunting pass over a market model. Extract only what the text actually says — every excerpt must be verbatim. Numeric claims are worth more than qualitative ones.

THE CURATED LEDGER NODES THIS GROUP FEEDS (with their derivations — compare against them):

   "method": "Capacity-split rescaling",

   "crossCheck": "Refinement cycle 1: NVIDIA's B300 reference architectures ship rack PDUs in both AC and DC variants (3 per rack / 160 per pod) — GPU growth is PDU-consuming. Value confirmed; downside band covers dense busbar deployments."
  },
  "provenance": {
   "rationale": "Single publisher's capacity series, but the 'GPU pods skip PDUs' doubt was refuted by vendor reference architectures (refinement cycle 1) → value confirmed with a downside band."

 }
]

RAW SEARCH RESULTS (group 'refine-boundary-substitution.enterprise-gpu-pods-skip-pdus', intent: NVIDIA's own DGX SuperPOD B300 Reference Architecture (dated 2025-10-30) shows the flagship GPU deployment shipping in standard EIA racks with three 2U rack PDUs each — a purchasable/published artifact that directly settles whether GPU racks skip PDUs today. Combined with the Grand View PDU market report attributing growth to AI/GPU workloads, the load-bearing premise ('GPU growth arrives PDU-less') is falsified now, not over time.):

# DGX SuperPOD Architecture#

The DGX SuperPOD architecture is a combination of DGX systems, Ethernet networking, InfiniBand Networking, management nodes, and storage. Figure 3 the rack layout of a single SU. With DGX SuperPOD B300, we introduce the MGX-based, DC busbar powered design for the best datacenter density and efficiency.

In our reference design, for DGX B300s are within a single rack. The rack-level power consumption per rack exceeds 50 kW. The rack layout can be adjusted to meet local data center requirements, such as maximum power per rack and rack layout between DGX systems and supporting equipment to meet local needs for power and cooling distribution.

For legacy datacenter without the possibility for DC busbar, it is still possible to build SuperPOD with traditional PDU and AC powered EIA racks.

Figure 3 shows 64 x NVIDIA DGX B300 Busbar systems in MGX racks each with DC Power shelfs and MGX rack stiffeners.

Figure 3 DGX B300 in Racks#

Figure 4 shows an example management rack configuration with networking switches, management servers, storage arrays, and UFM appliances. Sizes and quantities will vary depending upon models used.

Figure 4 Management rack configuration#

## Markdown Content:
DGX SuperPOD Architecture#

The DGX SuperPOD architecture is a combination of DGX systems, Ethernet networking, InfiniBand Networking, management nodes, and storage. Figure 2 the rack layout of a single SU. With DGX SuperPOD with DGX B300 systems, we utilize standard racks and with traditional power supplies and PDUs.

In our reference design, four DGX B300 fit within a single rack. The rack-level power consumption per rack is ~56kW. The rack layout can be adjusted to meet local data center requirements, such as maximum power per rack and rack layout between DGX systems and supporting equipment to meet local needs for power and cooling distribution.

This Reference Architecture is focused on traditional PDU and AC powered EIA racks. DGX SuperPOD with DGX B300 systems is also available for more dense DB Busbar solutions as well.

Figure 2 shows 72 x NVIDIA DGX B300 PS systems in standard racks each with three (3) 2U rack PDUs for maximum redundancy. Note that depending on your data center’s capability, you might need to reduce the number of DGXs hosted on the same rack.

Figure 2 DGX B300 in Racks#

     "title": "NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories",
     "url": "https://docs.nvidia.com/pdf/dgx-spod-gb300-ra.pdf",
     "publishedDate": null,
     "text": "## NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories

Reference Architecture Featuring NVIDIA DGX GB300 Systems

NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories | i

## Abstract

The NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD architecture has been designed to power the next generation AI factories with unparalleled performance, scalability, and innovation that supports all customers in the enterprise, higher education, research, and the public sectors. It is a physical twin of NVIDIA’s own system used by research and development teams, meaning the DGX SuperPOD customer’s infrastructure software, applications, and support have already been tested and vetted on the same architecture through NVIDIA’s internal usage.

This DGX SuperPOD Reference Architecture (RA) is based on DGX GB300 systems powered by Grace CPUs and Blackwell Ultra GPUs. The RA discusses the components that define the scalable and modular architecture of DGX SuperPOD. DGX SuperPOD is built on the concept of scalable units (SU); each SU contains 8 DGX GB300 systems, which enables rapid deployment of DGX SuperPOD of any size. The RA also includes details regarding the SU design and specifics of InfiniBand, NVLink network, Ethernet fabric topologies, storage system specifications, recommended rack layouts, and wiring guidelines.

NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories | ii

## Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit Market Summary

The global data center rack power distribution unit market size was estimated at USD 2.81 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 5.87 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 9.7% from 2026 to 2033. The growth of high-performance computing (HPC), artificial intelligence (AI), and GPU-intensive workloads is driving demand for data center rack power distribution unit market.

### Key Market Trends & Insights

- North America held 38.0% revenue share of the global data center rack power distribution unit market.
- In the U.S., the expansion of colocation data centers is accelerating the demand for data center rack power distribution unit market.
- By rack type, non-intelligent segment held the largest revenue share in 2025.

### Market Size & Forecast

- 2025 Market Size: USD 2.81 Billion
- 2033 Projected Market Size: USD 5.87 Billion
- CAGR (2026-2033): 9.7%
- North America: Largest market in 2025

### DATA CENTER PDU MARKET SIZE, SHARE & TRENDS

According to Marketsandmarkets, the data center PDU market size was valued USD 3.94 biliion in 2025 and is project to reach USD 14.23 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 19.5% from 2026 to 2032. The data center PDU market is driven by the expansion of hyperscale and AI-driven data centers, the increasing rack power density, and the rising adoption of intelligent PDUs for real-time monitoring and operational efficiency. Additionally, the strong focus on energy efficiency, sustainability, and reliable power distribution infrastructure is further supporting the demand for advanced PDU solutions across modern data center environments.

### MARKET SNAPSHOT TABLE

- Schneider Electric (France)
- Eaton (Ireland)
- Vertiv Group Corp. (US)
- Legrand (France)
- ABB (Switzerland)
- nVent (UK)

{"claims":[{"claim":"NVIDIA's AC-powered DGX B300 reference architecture ships 72 DGX B300 systems in standard racks, each with three 2U rack PDUs","number":3,"unit":"rack PDUs per rack","sourceTitle":"DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD with DGX B300 Systems, NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand switching and AC Power Reference Architecture","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/dgx-superpod/reference-architecture/scalable-infrastructure-b300-xdr/latest/dgx-superpod-architecture.html","excerpt":"Figure 2 shows 72 x NVIDIA DGX B300 PS systems in standard racks each with three (3) 2U rack PDUs for maximum redundancy.","tags":["structure","substitution"]},{"claim":"NVIDIA explicitly frames the AC/PDU architecture as the traditional option, distinct from a DC busbar alternative","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD with DGX B300 Systems, NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand switching and AC Power Reference Architecture","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/dgx-superpod/reference-architecture/scalable-infrastructure-b300-xdr/latest/dgx-superpod-architecture.html","excerpt":"This Reference Architecture is focused on traditional PDU and AC powered EIA racks. DGX SuperPOD with DGX B300 systems is also available for more dense DB Busbar solutions as well.","tags":["structure","substitution"]},{"claim":"NVIDIA's DC busbar reference architecture is marketed as the higher-density, higher-efficiency alternative that bypasses traditional per-rack AC PDU design","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/dgx-superpod/reference-architecture/scalable-infrastructure-b300/latest/dgx-superpod-architecture.html","excerpt":"With DGX SuperPOD B300, we introduce the MGX-based, DC busbar powered design for the best datacenter density and efficiency.","tags":["structure","substitution"]},{"claim":"NVIDIA states legacy datacenters without DC busbar capability can still build with traditional PDU and AC powered racks, implying PDU racks are the fallback/legacy option not the flagship","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/dgx-superpod/reference-architecture/scalable-infrastructure-b300/latest/dgx-superpod-architecture.html","excerpt":"For legacy datacenter without the possibility for DC busbar, it is still possible to build SuperPOD with traditional PDU and AC powered EIA racks.","tags":["structure","substitution"]},{"claim":"Even NVIDIA's DC busbar bill-of-materials includes 160 rack PDUs across 80 MGX racks (72 with busbar, 8 without), so busbar deployments are not fully PDU-less","number":160,"unit":"rPDU units","sourceTitle":"Major Components — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/dgx-superpod/reference-architecture/scalable-infrastructure-b300/latest/components.html","excerpt":"160
rPDU for MGX rack
PX4-57A7I2U-C8E7V2","tags":["structure","substitution"]},{"claim":"NVIDIA's DC busbar architecture bill of materials includes 72 MGX racks with busbar and 8 MGX racks without busbar","number":72,"unit":"racks with busbar (of 80 total)","sourceTitle":"Major Components — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/dgx-superpod/reference-architecture/scalable-infrastructure-b300/latest/components.html","excerpt":"72
19” MGX rack with bubar
754-24972-0046-001
8
19” MGX rack without busbar
754-24972-0047-001","tags":["structure"]},{"claim":"NVIDIA's DC busbar reference design puts rack-level power consumption above 50kW per rack","number":50,"unit":"kW per rack (exceeds)","sourceTitle":"DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD DGX B300 Systems, Spectrum-4 Ethernet and DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/dgx-superpod/reference-architecture/scalable-infrastructure-b300/latest/dgx-superpod-architecture.html","excerpt":"The rack-level power consumption per rack exceeds 50 kW.","tags":["structure"]},{"claim":"NVIDIA's AC/PDU reference design puts rack-level power consumption at approximately 56kW per rack","number":56,"unit":"kW per rack","sourceTitle":"DGX SuperPOD Architecture — NVIDIA SuperPOD with DGX B300 Systems, NVIDIA Quantum-X800 InfiniBand switching and AC Power Reference Architecture","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/dgx-superpod/reference-architecture/scalable-infrastructure-b300-xdr/latest/dgx-superpod-architecture.html","excerpt":"In our reference design, four DGX B300 fit within a single rack. The rack-level power consumption per rack is ~56kW.","tags":["structure"]},{"claim":"Grand View Research sizes the global data center rack PDU market at $2.81B in 2025, growing to $5.87B by 2033 at 9.7% CAGR, driven by AI/GPU workloads","number":2.81,"unit":"USD billion (2025)","sourceTitle":"Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit Market Report 2033","url":"https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/data-center-rack-power-distribution-unit-pdu-market","excerpt":"The global data center rack power distribution unit market size was estimated at USD 2.81 billion in 2025 and is projected to reach USD 5.87 billion by 2033, growing at a CAGR of 9.7% from 2026 to 2033. The growth of high-performance computing (HPC), artificial intelligence (AI), and GPU-intensive workloads is driving demand for data center rack power distribution unit market.","tags":["demand","base-rate"]},{"claim":"Grand View reports non-intelligent PDUs held the largest revenue share of the rack PDU market in 2025","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit Market Report 2033","url":"https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/data-center-rack-power-distribution-unit-pdu-market","excerpt":"By rack type, non-intelligent segment held the largest revenue share in 2025.","tags":["structure","base-rate"]},{"claim":"North America held 38.0% revenue share of the global rack PDU market","number":38,"unit":"% revenue share","sourceTitle":"Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit Market Report 2033","url":"https://www.grandviewresearch.com/industry-analysis/data-center-rack-power-distribution-unit-pdu-market","excerpt":"North America held 38.0% revenue share of the global data center rack power distribution unit market.","tags":["base-rate","structure"]},{"claim":"Mordor Intelligence sizes the rack PDU market at $2.78B in 2025 rising to $3.01B in 2026 and $4.62B by 2031 at 8.96% CAGR, citing hyperscale expansion, high-density AI workloads, and liquid cooling adoption","number":2.78,"unit":"USD billion (2025)","sourceTitle":"Data Center Rack Power Distribution Unit (PDU) Market Size, Share, Trends","url":"https://www.mordorintelligence.com/industry-reports/data-center-rack-pdu-market","excerpt":"The data center rack power distribution unit (PDU) market size is expected to increase from USD 2.78 billion in 2025 to USD 3.01 billion in 2026 and reach USD 4.62 billion by 2031, growing at a CAGR of 8.96% over 2026-2031. Momentum stems from hyperscale expansion, high-density AI workloads, and liquid-cooling adoption","tags":["demand","base-rate"]},{"claim":"MarketsAndMarkets sizes the broader data center PDU market at $3.94B in 2025 growing to $14.23B by 2032 at 19.5% CAGR, and segments the market by AI Workload vs General-Purpose IT Workload","number":3.94,"unit":"USD billion (2025)","sourceTitle":"Data Center PDU Market Size, Share, Latest Trends & Growth Analysis, 2025-2032","url":"https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/data-center-pdu-market-183927907.html","excerpt":"According to Marketsandmarkets, the data center PDU market size was valued USD 3.94 biliion in 2025 and is project to reach USD 14.23 billion by 2032, growing at a CAGR of 19.5% from 2026 to 2032. The data center PDU market is driven by the expansion of hyperscale and AI-driven data centers, the increasing rack power density","tags":["demand","base-rate","structure"]},{"claim":"MarketsAndMarkets segments the PDU market by end-user including Hyperscale, Colocation, and Enterprise categories","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"Data Center PDU Market Size, Share, Latest Trends & Growth Analysis, 2025-2032","url":"https://www.marketsandmarkets.com/Market-Reports/data-center-pdu-market-183927907.html","excerpt":"Data Center PDU Market Size, Share & Trends by Product Level (Rack PDU, Floor PDU), Phase Type (Single-Phase, Three-Phase), Workload Type (Al Workload, General-Purpose IT Workload), End User (Hyperscale, Colocation, Enterprise) - Global Forecast to 2032","tags":["structure","competition"]},{"claim":"Omdia's Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis 2025 uses 2024 as a base year with historical data back to 2017 and forecasts to 2030, but full figures are paywalled","number":null,"unit":null,"sourceTitle":"Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis – 2025 Omdia","url":"https://omdia.tech.informa.com/om136696/rack-pdu-annual-market-analysis--2025","excerpt":"Analyzes the Rack PDU Annual Tracker – 2025 update and includes forecast and vendor shares. Uses 2024 as a base year, with historical data back to 2017 and forecasts to 2030.","tags":["base-rate","execution"]},{"claim":"NVIDIA's DGX GB300 SuperPOD reference architecture is built on scalable units, with each scalable unit containing 8 DGX GB300 systems","number":8,"unit":"DGX GB300 systems per scalable unit","sourceTitle":"NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD: Next Generation Scalable Infrastructure for AI Factories","url":"https://docs.nvidia.com/pdf/dgx-spod-gb300-ra.pdf","excerpt":"DGX SuperPOD is built on the concept of scalable units (SU); each SU contains 8 DGX GB300 systems, which enables rapid deployment of DGX SuperPOD of any size.","tags":["structure","definition"]}],"tensions":[{"text":"The curated node's refinement cycle claims the 'GPU pods skip PDUs' doubt was refuted and the value 'confirmed' by vendor reference architectures, but NVIDIA actually publishes parallel architectures: an AC/PDU version (legacy/fallback) and a DC busbar version explicitly marketed as the denser, more efficient flagship design — meaning the highest-density GPU deployments are steered toward bypassing traditional per-rack AC PDUs rather than confirming universal PDU attach.","nodes":["seg.enterprise"]},{"text":"Even NVIDIA's own 'DC Busbar Power Reference Architecture' bill of materials lists 160 rack PDUs across 80 MGX racks, so the refutation is only partial: busbar racks still consume some rPDUs, but at a materially lower ratio than the AC architecture's 3 PDUs per rack for 72 racks — a nuance the node's binary 'confirmed' framing elides.","nodes":["seg.enterprise"]},{"text":"Three independent PDU market-size estimates for the same base year (2025) diverge meaningfully: Grand View ($2.81B, 9.7% CAGR to 2033), Mordor Intelligence ($2.78B, 8.96% CAGR to 2031), and MarketsAndMarkets ($3.94B, 19.5% CAGR to 2032) — the underlying market definition and growth trajectory are not well triangulated despite similar 2025 base figures.","nodes":["seg.enterprise"]}],"absences":["No source quantifies the actual share (%) of deployed/shipped GPU racks using AC rack-PDU architecture versus DC busbar architecture in the field — only vendor reference-architecture options were found, not real-world adoption shares.","Omdia's Rack PDU Annual Market Analysis 2025, Rack PDU Market Tracker 2025, and Rack PDU Annual Tracker 2024 — the reports most likely to contain vendor-share and AI/GPU-driven attach-rate breakdowns — are paywalled, so no numeric attach-rate or vendor-share figures were retrievable.","No query result quantifies what fraction of the $2.81B–$3.94B PDU market (2025) is specifically attributable to AI/GPU workloads versus general-purpose IT, despite multiple reports naming AI/GPU as a growth driver.","No data found on real-world enterprise on-premise adoption rates of DC busbar power versus traditional rack PDUs, as opposed to hyperscale/AI-factory deployments where the NVIDIA reference architectures are aimed."]}
